title: pingraea viscosa
intro: Pingraea viscosa, also known as the sticky bush, is a shrubby plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its sticky or resinous leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pingraea viscosa include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and has also been used in topical applications to promote wound healing and skin health.
